title: Erdős Conjecture on Arithmetic Progressions
statement_text: Any subset $A$ of $\mathbb{N}$ with divergent harmonic sum, i.e., $\sum_{a \in A} 1/a = \infty$, must contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.
context_text: Erdős conjectured that the density of the primes alone should guarantee the existence of long APs. The Green-Tao theorem confirms the case $A = $ primes (since $\sum_p 1/p$ diverges), but the general conjecture remains open and has not been proved even for 3-term APs.
paper_contribution: This exposition of the Green-Tao theorem establishes a special case of the conjecture: the prime numbers, whose reciprocal sum diverges, contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction without a labelled theorem environment; PDF extraction — math rendered from Unicode.
